content: Cait: Hey, Professor, what are you doing here this late? Professor Scara: Oh, Cait. I'm just working on some robotics research. How about you? What brings you to the Science! Center in the middle of the night? Cait: Just passing through. I heard you're pretty knowledgeable about robots. I'm more interested in the humanoid ones, if you know what I mean.  You ever experiment with them, Scara? Professor Scara: Uh, well, my work is strictly academic, Cait. I have no interest in... unorthodox applications of my research. Cait: Come on, Scara, don't be a prude. We all know what kind of things go on in the dark corners of this world. You've got to be curious about, you know, the possibilities. Professor Scara: Cait, please.

Professor Scara: Listen, Cait, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but my research has its limitations. And right now, my priority is understanding the workings of these robots, not their... intimate applications. Just please respect that. Cait: Sure, Scara. I guess you're just more traditional than me. You know what they say about robots... They're just programmed to please, after all.
